what_it_does: Implements a propose-only flow that checks a platform binary's live version against a stored version stamp, re-introspects and diffs the capability surface when versions differ, and surfaces a single governance inbox item carrying the full batch diff; it deliberately does not write the stamp or cache itself.
observations: FLOW registration dict at module top with id cc_registry_refresh and proposes_only: True.
run() is registry-driven: it looks up platform_id in PlatformRegistry and can refresh any discoverable platform row.
Same-version fast path returns immediately with status unchanged and zero surface() calls.
Uses injected discover_fn and executable parameters to enable testing without spawning a live binary.
Diff payload includes added, changed, unclassified, vanished, version_from, and version_to.
run_all() iterates every registered platform that declares a version_source and isolates failures per platform.
write_stamp_and_cache() is exported for a separate post-approval governance step and is not called by run().
Reads and writes flat operational files in company/store/ rather than a CAS store.
Corrupt cache rows and missing stamps are handled gracefully by treating the prior snapshot as empty.
Uses _engine.refresh() to perform DISCOVER+CLASSIFY and compute the diff against the prior CapabilityEntry list.
standouts: Propose-only discipline: the flow surfaces a gap but deliberately never advances the version stamp or cache.
Registry-driven platform selection with run_all() automatically covering all version-source platforms.
Single surface() call per version bump carrying the full batch payload rather than one item per changed capability.
conventions: Follows a flow registration pattern with a top-level FLOW dict including id, label, description, params, and proposes_only.
Uses module-level ROOT computed from __file__ for store paths.
Separates read/write of operational flat files from content-addressed storage.
Fail-closed: missing or unreachable version raises RuntimeError; surfaced item uses default=reject.
Testability via dependency injection of discover_fn and executable rather than monkeypatching.
Breaks pure-function convention by mutating sys.path inside functions and by performing side-effecting surface() calls.
concerns: sys.path.insert(0, ROOT) is called repeatedly inside functions, which can duplicate entries and leak import path state.
Broad except Exception in run_all() and silent dropping of corrupt cache rows swallow details that might aid debugging.
_engine.refresh() returns a payload dict containing a private _current key that is popped before surfacing, creating a coupling risk.
run() returns a public result dict that includes the private-ish key _new_entries_for_post_approval_cache.
write_stamp_and_cache writes the same introspection_cache.json for every platform, so multi-platform caches would collide unless platform-specific paths are intended.
notes: This is the propose-only, version-triggered capability-registry refresh flow that surfaces one batched governance gap per version bump and leaves stamp and cache writes to post-approval governance.
questions: What is the exact shape and schema of CapabilityEntry and the diff payload produced by _engine.refresh()?
How does the governance flow invoke write_stamp_and_cache() and ensure consistency between the stamp and cache writes?
What does PlatformRegistry.discover() return and how are platforms/<id>.py modules loaded?
What is the contract of Inbox.surface() and how is default=reject interpreted by consumers?
Is introspection_cache.json intended to be global or per-platform, given write_stamp_and_cache always uses the same path?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is propose-only, surfaces a single batched gap on version mismatch, and does not advance the stamp or cache.
apparent_themes: Operational flat files versus content-addressed storage
intention_signals: It is trying to automate detection of capability drift across external platform binaries while keeping a human curator in the loop: the flow notices version changes, gathers a complete diff, and asks for approval before updating the trusted registry snapshot.
novelty: The deliberate refusal to write the stamp or cache inside the detection flow, exporting a separate write function for post-approval governance, and the single batched surface() call per version bump rather than one item per changed capability.
contribution: A reusable, propose-only refresh pattern that separates drift detection from trusted-state mutation, supports multiple platforms through a registry, and batches the resulting diff into one governance inbox item.
summary_for_embedding: company/flows/cc_registry_refresh.py is a propose-only flow that compares a platform binary's live version to a stored stamp, re-introspects and diffs capabilities on mismatch, and surfaces a single batched cc_registry_gap inbox item, leaving version-stamp and snapshot-cache updates to a post-approval governance step.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to detect external capability-surface drift per platform version bump and queue a single curator-approved update to the trusted registry.
suggested_fields: approval_hook: the governance action or handler that calls write_stamp_and_cache
platform_scope: whether a flow targets a single platform or all registered platforms
propose_only_marker: explicit boolean tag for flows that only surface proposals
diff_schema: schema of the payload keys surfaced
proposed_edge_kinds: approves_via: flow surfaces an item that is later approved by a governance action
writes_after_approval: function intended to be invoked only after a separate approval step
registry_selects: flow dynamically selects its target entity from PlatformRegistry
injects_for_test: parameter exists solely to enable test substitution
fail_closed_against: default outcome when a curator does not respond

symbols.does: Returns the canonical path to the company/store/ directory.
symbols.description: A small helper that builds the flat operational store path from the module ROOT. It is used by stamp and cache path helpers.

symbols.does: Returns the per-platform version stamp file path.
symbols.description: Builds store/<platform_id>.version_stamp. The file is treated as a flat operational file, not a CAS object.

symbols.does: Returns the path to the prior capability snapshot cache.
symbols.description: Builds store/introspection_cache.json, which holds the previously classified capability list for diffing.

symbols.does: Reads the stored version stamp, returning an empty string if missing.
symbols.description: A missing stamp signals first-run behavior and triggers a full re-introspection rather than a same-version no-op.

symbols.does: Reads the prior capability snapshot, returning an empty list if missing or corrupt.
symbols.description: Corrupt cache data is treated as an empty prior set, so the new snapshot effectively replaces it after approval.

symbols.does: Resolves the platform executable and probes its live version.
symbols.description: Uses introspection.discover helpers to find the binary and run its version command. Fails loud if the output is empty.

symbols.does: Main flow entry point that detects version drift and surfaces a batched gap.
symbols.description: Resolves the platform from PlatformRegistry, compares stored and live versions, calls _engine.refresh() to diff capabilities, pops the private _current snapshot, surfaces one cc_registry_gap inbox item, and returns status plus payload. It never writes the stamp or cache.

symbols.does: Surfaces a governance inbox item with the diff payload.
symbols.description: Opens the FsStore-backed Inbox and calls surface(action_class, payload, default=reject), returning the surfaced item id.

symbols.does: Writes the version stamp and snapshot cache after curator approval.
symbols.description: Exported for the post-approval governance step. It writes store/<platform_id>.version_stamp and store/introspection_cache.json, advancing the trusted state only after a gap has been approved.
